title: Ambient Diffusion Policy: Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Data in Robotics
summary: Ambient Diffusion Policy is a simple and principled method for imitation learning from suboptimal data in robotics. Whereas existing methods that co-train on high-quality and suboptimal demonstrations often fail to separate the meaningful and harmful features in the suboptimal samples, it extracts only the useful features by introducing a new axis to co-training: noise-dependent data usage — restricting the contribution of suboptimal data during training to only the high and low diffusion times. This exploits two properties induced by the spectral power law of robot action data — a global-to-local hierarchy and locality. Across four types of suboptimal data (noisy trajectories, sim-to-real gap, task mismatch, and large-scale data mixtures) over six tasks it effectively learns from arbitrary sources, notably outperforming existing co-training baselines by up to 33% when scaled to Open X-Embodiment.
